---
title: "Introduction to B2CViz"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Introduction to B2CViz}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

# Introduction

B2CViz is a package designed to help visualize VisiumHD spatial single-cell data that has been segmented with bin2cell. This vignette will guide you through the basic usage of the package.

# Setup

The following libraries are required: 'jpeg', 'png', 'tiff', 'Seurat', 'dplyr', 'ggplot2', 'ggrepel', 'imager', 'shiny', 'ggnewscale', 'tidyr'

# Loading Data

For this example, we'll assume you have processed Seurat objects with spatial data and the associated image file used for the bin2cell segmentation. Here's how you would typically load your data:

``` r
# Load your Seurat objects
obj_pre <- readRDS("/path/to/your/seurat_object.rds") # pre-aggregated object
obj_post <- readRDS("/path/to/your/seurat_object.rds") # post-aggregated object

# Specify the path to the HD image file used by bin2cell (works with jpg or png)
image_path <- "/path/to/your/image.jpg"

# Create a bin2cell (B2C) object
b2c <- load_b2c(pre = object_pre, post = object_post, path = image_path)
```

# Selecting a Region of Interest (ROI)

To select a region of interest, use the set_roi function:

``` r
# set region of interest
b2c <- set_roi(b2c = b2c)
```

This will open an interactive Shiny app where you can select your ROIs.

# Plotting Features

To display the whole slide, use the scaledown and overview_b2c functions.

``` r
b2c <- scaledown_img(b2c = b2c)
overview_b2c(b2c = b2c, feat = "CDH1")
```

To visualize cell features at higher resolution, crop the region of interest and call plot_b2c function.

``` r
# crop b2c object using ROI limits
b2c_1 <- crop_b2c(b2c, roi = 1)
b2c_2 <- crop_b2c(b2c, roi = 2)

# plot ROIs
plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_1, feat = "CDH1")
plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_2, feat = "CDH1")
```

Adjust parameters as needed (cells or H&E transparency, counts threshold for visibility, plotting colors etc) 

``` r
plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_1,
         feat = "CDH1",
         alpha.low = 0.1,
         min.visible = 2,
         he_alpha = 0.1,
         col.high = "orangered",
         outline.hulls = c(149249, 186039))
```

Compute cell-cell distances from plots

``` r
p <- plot_b2c(b2c = b2c_1, feat = c("CDH1", "CD4"), plot = F)
output_data <- get_dist(p)
plot_dist(output_data)
```
